---
title: "What did feeder cattle bring at Clovis, by weight?"
subtitle: "Price–weight explorer · Clovis, NM auction"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**Caveat before scrolling.** Clovis is one auction in one region. These prices are not representative of national feeder-cattle prices. Treat this page as a regional reference, not a market index. See [Methodology](methodology/index.qmd) for the data window and deflation choice.
:::

## About this page

This page shows weekly feeder-cattle pricing by weight class at the Clovis, NM auction, drawn from USDA-AMS Market News and refreshed automatically each week by the `clovis_refresh` GitHub Actions workflow. Two button rows above the chart let you pick a **year window** — All, Last 5 years, Last 3 years, or the latest calendar year — in either **nominal dollars** (top row) or **real December 2025 dollars** (bottom row). Only one button is highlighted at a time across both rows; clicking any button sets the chart to that combination. The chart is intentionally focused: a single auction, a single grade combination, with the producer's "what does a heavier calf bring this week vs. recent years?" question as the question it answers.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: data-load

# Load Clovis observations via the shared loader at pipelines/clovis/load.py.
# The loader unions the live MARS-era parquet (clovis_latest.parquet, refreshed
# weekly by the clovis_refresh GitHub Actions workflow) with the one-time
# historical Era B block (clovis_historical_era_b_latest.parquet, ingested
# 2026-04-25 from USDA-AMS Market News archive: 2017-10-04 → 2019-04-10).
# The dedupe rule is most-recent-vintage-wins on
# (auction_date, class, frame, muscle_grade, weight_break_low, weight_break_high);
# in practice the era windows are disjoint so no dedupes fire.
# If neither file exists, fall back to a clearly-labelled synthetic stand-in
# so the page layout remains honest while not claiming to show real prices.

from pathlib import Path
import sys
import pandas as pd

PROCESSED = Path("..") / "data" / "processed"
CPI_PATH = PROCESSED / "cpi_latest.parquet"

# Make pipelines/ importable when Quarto runs this chunk from site/.
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path("..").resolve()))
from pipelines.clovis.load import load_clovis_combined  # noqa: E402

data_is_placeholder = False
data_source_label = None

_loaded = load_clovis_combined(PROCESSED)
if _loaded is not None:
    df = _loaded.df
    data_source_label = _loaded.data_source_label
else:
    # Synthetic stand-in. Clearly labelled so it is never mistaken for real data.
    import numpy as np
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed=2026)
    weight_bins = ["300-399 lb", "400-499 lb", "500-599 lb", "600-699 lb", "700-799 lb", "800-899 lb"]
    years = list(range(2020, 2026))
    classes = ["Steers", "Heifers"]
    rows = []
    base_by_bin = {wb: 280 - i * 15 for i, wb in enumerate(weight_bins)}
    for year in years:
        for wb in weight_bins:
            for cl in classes:
                mean = base_by_bin[wb] + rng.normal(0, 8) + (year - 2020) * 5
                if cl == "Heifers":
                    mean -= 12
                rows.append({
                    "auction_date": pd.Timestamp(f"{year}-06-15"),
                    "weight_bin": wb,
                    "class": cl,
                    "head_count": int(rng.integers(40, 400)),
                    "price_low": round(mean - 10, 2),
                    "price_avg": round(mean, 2),
                    "price_high": round(mean + 10, 2),
                })
    df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
    data_is_placeholder = True
    data_source_label = "synthetic stand-in (no live data available)"

if data_is_placeholder:
    print("SYNTHETIC STAND-IN DATA — for layout only. Not real prices.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: data-shape

# Normalize the parquet into the long-format fields the chart needs.
#
# The live MARS snapshot preserves 50-lb weight bins (weight_break_low 300,
# 350, 400, ...). The chart aggregates to 100-lb bins (300-399, 400-499, ...) —
# the conventional aggregation in USDA-AMS Market News and producer-facing
# livestock publications, and the granularity at which buyers and sellers
# negotiate. We collapse the 50-lb MARS bins into 100-lb bins, filter to
# the dominant grade combination at this auction (Medium and Large,
# muscle_grade 1), and aggregate across the full available window.

import pandas as pd

if not data_is_placeholder:
    slim = df[
        (df["class"].isin(["Steers", "Heifers"]))
        & (df["frame"] == "Medium and Large")
        & (df["muscle_grade"] == "1")
    ].copy()
    # MARS started reporting weight_break_low/high consistently in 2024. For
    # earlier years we derive the bin from avg_weight. MARS's 2024+ bins are
    # 50-lb wide (300,350,400,...); we collapse both branches to 100-lb bins
    # (300,400,500,...) so one observation in a 350–399 lb MARS bin lands
    # in the same chart bar as a 2019 observation of a 370 lb pen.
    wb = slim["weight_break_low"]
    aw = slim["avg_weight"]
    raw_weight = wb.fillna(aw)
    slim = slim[raw_weight.notna()].copy()
    slim["bin_lo"] = ((raw_weight[raw_weight.notna()] // 100) * 100).astype(int)
    slim = slim[(slim["bin_lo"] >= 300) & (slim["bin_lo"] <= 800)]
    slim["bin_hi"] = slim["bin_lo"] + 99
    slim["weight_bin"] = slim.apply(
        lambda r: f"{int(r['bin_lo'])}–{int(r['bin_hi'])} lb", axis=1
    )
    work = slim[
        ["auction_date", "weight_bin", "class", "head_count",
         "price_low", "price_avg", "price_high"]
    ].copy()
else:
    work = df  # placeholder already has the right columns

# Window metadata for the caption
date_min = pd.to_datetime(work["auction_date"]).min()
date_max = pd.to_datetime(work["auction_date"]).max()
n_dates = pd.to_datetime(work["auction_date"]).dt.date.nunique()
n_obs = len(work)
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: deflate-to-dec-2025

# CPI deflation. The BLS pipeline (pipelines/bls/) writes cpi_latest.parquet
# with columns period (month-start date), cpi_u (All Items NSA), vintage,
# revision_count. The site's real-dollar basis is December 2025 — a fixed
# month chosen at Phase 1 release so historical chart values stay stable
# across BLS revisions. The deflator for an observation in month M is:
#
#     deflator_M = cpi_u(Dec 2025) / cpi_u(M)
#     price_real_M = price_nominal_M * deflator_M
#
# If cpi_latest.parquet isn't present (e.g. a transient rendering run before
# the first BLS backfill), real-dollar columns are set equal to nominal and
# the page still renders; the toggle below just shows identical curves.

import pandas as pd

BASIS_PERIOD = pd.Timestamp("2025-12-01")
cpi_basis_label = "December 2025"

deflation_available = False
if CPI_PATH.exists():
    cpi = pd.read_parquet(CPI_PATH)[["period", "cpi_u"]].copy()
    cpi["period"] = pd.to_datetime(cpi["period"])
    basis_rows = cpi[cpi["period"] == BASIS_PERIOD]
    if not basis_rows.empty:
        cpi_basis_value = float(basis_rows["cpi_u"].iloc[0])
        deflation_available = True
    else:
        # Fall back to the latest CPI month if December 2025 isn't published yet.
        fallback = cpi.sort_values("period").iloc[-1]
        cpi_basis_value = float(fallback["cpi_u"])
        cpi_basis_label = pd.to_datetime(fallback["period"]).strftime("%B %Y")
        deflation_available = True

if deflation_available:
    # Join each observation to its month's CPI.
    work["period_month"] = work["auction_date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
    cpi_lookup = cpi.set_index("period")["cpi_u"]
    work["cpi_u_month"] = work["period_month"].map(cpi_lookup)
    work["deflator"] = cpi_basis_value / work["cpi_u_month"]
    # Observations whose month has not yet published a CPI value (typically
    # the trailing month or two — BLS publishes with a ~2-week lag, and
    # Clovis auctions are reported immediately) fall back to deflator = 1
    # so those rows render as nominal in the real view. Logged to stderr so
    # CI keeps the count but the rendered page does not leak the message.
    import sys as _sys
    missing_cpi = int(work["deflator"].isna().sum())
    if missing_cpi:
        print(
            f"[deflate] {missing_cpi} observations lack a CPI month-match; "
            "leaving nominal.",
            file=_sys.stderr,
        )
    work["deflator"] = work["deflator"].fillna(1.0)
    work["price_low_real"] = work["price_low"] * work["deflator"]
    work["price_avg_real"] = work["price_avg"] * work["deflator"]
    work["price_high_real"] = work["price_high"] * work["deflator"]
else:
    # No CPI available — real columns equal nominal so the toggle is harmless.
    work["price_low_real"] = work["price_low"]
    work["price_avg_real"] = work["price_avg"]
    work["price_high_real"] = work["price_high"]
```

::: {.callout-important}
**Data source**: `{python} data_source_label` ·
**Window**: `{python} date_min.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')` to `{python} date_max.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')` ·
**Auction dates**: `{python} n_dates` ·
**Observations**: `{python} f"{n_obs:,}"` ·
**Grade shown**: Medium and Large, muscle grade 1 (the dominant grade combination at this auction) ·
**Deflation basis**: `{python} cpi_basis_label` CPI-U NSA (`CUUR0000SA0`).
:::

## Cash price–weight relationships

Two panels, one per class — Steers on top, Heifers below — each showing the envelope of prices observed in the selected window. The gray band spans the Minimum and Maximum across the window; the black line is the Average. Two button rows above the panels let the reader pick the **year window** (All, Last 5 years, Last 3 years, or Latest calendar year) and the **dollar basis** (nominal or real Dec 2025); each click sets a new view, defaulting the other dimension to its baseline. Hover any point to see the exact values and the head count behind that weight class.

```{python}
#| echo: false

import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# 4 year-windows × 2 modes = 8 view combinations × 6 traces per view (3 per
# class × 2 classes) = 48 total traces. The user picks one combination at a
# time via two button groups; each click resets visibility to the chosen
# (year, mode) tuple. Switching one dimension resets the other to its
# baseline (year=All, mode=nominal).

# ---------- year windows ----------
# Anchor on the data's most recent observation rather than today, so the
# windows are stable on weeks where the auction is dark or the BLS pipeline
# lags.
_ad = pd.to_datetime(work["auction_date"]) if not data_is_placeholder else pd.to_datetime(work["auction_date"])
_anchor = _ad.max()
_anchor_year = int(_anchor.year)

YEAR_WINDOWS = [
    ("all",    "All",            _ad.min(),                                     _anchor),
    ("5y",     "Last 5 years",   _anchor - pd.DateOffset(years=5),              _anchor),
    ("3y",     "Last 3 years",   _anchor - pd.DateOffset(years=3),              _anchor),
    ("latest", f"{_anchor_year}", pd.Timestamp(_anchor_year, 1, 1),             _anchor),
]


def _aggregate(work_df, low_col, avg_col, high_col):
    agg = (
        work_df.groupby(["weight_bin", "class"], as_index=False)
               .agg(
                   price_low=(low_col, "min"),
                   price_mean=(avg_col, "mean"),
                   price_high=(high_col, "max"),
                   head_count=("head_count", "sum"),
               )
    )
    bin_order = sorted(
        agg["weight_bin"].unique(),
        key=lambda s: int(s.split("–")[0].split("-")[0]),
    )
    agg["weight_bin"] = pd.Categorical(agg["weight_bin"], categories=bin_order, ordered=True)
    return agg.sort_values(["class", "weight_bin"])


# ---------- panels and trace styling ----------
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("NM Clovis — Steers", "NM Clovis — Heifers"),
)

BAND_FILL = "rgba(170,170,170,0.35)"
BAND_EDGE = "rgba(170,170,170,0.0)"
AVG_LINE = "#1a1a1a"
HOVER_FMT = (
    "<b>%{x}</b><br>"
    "Min: $%{customdata[0]:.2f}<br>"
    "Avg: $%{customdata[1]:.2f}<br>"
    "Max: $%{customdata[2]:.2f}<br>"
    "Head: %{customdata[3]:,}<extra></extra>"
)


def _add_class_traces(agg_df, cls, row, name_suffix, visible):
    sub = agg_df[agg_df["class"] == cls]
    if sub.empty:
        # Emit empty traces so visibility-mask indices stay predictable
        for _ in range(3):
            fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=[], y=[], mode="lines",
                                     showlegend=False, hoverinfo="skip", visible=visible),
                          row=row, col=1)
        return
    x = sub["weight_bin"].astype(str).tolist()
    lo, avg, hi = sub["price_low"].tolist(), sub["price_mean"].tolist(), sub["price_high"].tolist()
    cd = list(zip(lo, avg, hi, sub["head_count"].tolist()))
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(x=x, y=lo, mode="lines", line=dict(color=BAND_EDGE, width=0),
                   showlegend=False, hoverinfo="skip", visible=visible,
                   name=f"{cls} min ({name_suffix})"),
        row=row, col=1,
    )
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(x=x, y=hi, mode="lines", line=dict(color=BAND_EDGE, width=0),
                   fill="tonexty", fillcolor=BAND_FILL,
                   showlegend=False, hoverinfo="skip", visible=visible,
                   name=f"{cls} max ({name_suffix})"),
        row=row, col=1,
    )
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(x=x, y=avg, mode="lines+markers",
                   line=dict(color=AVG_LINE, width=2),
                   marker=dict(color=AVG_LINE, size=6),
                   name=f"{cls} avg ({name_suffix})",
                   showlegend=False, visible=visible,
                   customdata=cd, hovertemplate=HOVER_FMT),
        row=row, col=1,
    )


# ---------- emit 8 trace groups, default visible = (All, Nominal) ----------
# Trace order (so the visibility vector is predictable):
#   For each year_window in YEAR_WINDOWS:
#       For each mode in (nominal, real):
#           Steers traces (3), then Heifers traces (3)  -> 6 traces per group
# So group index g = year_idx * 2 + mode_idx; first trace at index g*6.
N_TRACES_PER_GROUP = 6
N_GROUPS = len(YEAR_WINDOWS) * 2  # 8
N_TRACES = N_GROUPS * N_TRACES_PER_GROUP  # 48

for y_idx, (y_key, y_label, lo_d, hi_d) in enumerate(YEAR_WINDOWS):
    sub = work[(pd.to_datetime(work["auction_date"]) >= lo_d)
               & (pd.to_datetime(work["auction_date"]) <= hi_d)]
    agg_n = _aggregate(sub, "price_low", "price_avg", "price_high")
    agg_r = _aggregate(sub, "price_low_real", "price_avg_real", "price_high_real")
    is_default = (y_idx == 0)  # only year=All & mode=nominal visible by default
    _add_class_traces(agg_n, "Steers",  row=1, name_suffix=f"{y_label}, nominal",  visible=is_default)
    _add_class_traces(agg_n, "Heifers", row=2, name_suffix=f"{y_label}, nominal",  visible=is_default)
    _add_class_traces(agg_r, "Steers",  row=1, name_suffix=f"{y_label}, real",     visible=False)
    _add_class_traces(agg_r, "Heifers", row=2, name_suffix=f"{y_label}, real",     visible=False)


def _visibility_for(year_idx: int, mode_idx: int) -> list[bool]:
    """Build a 48-long visibility mask for the chosen (year, mode) tuple."""
    out = [False] * N_TRACES
    group = year_idx * 2 + mode_idx
    start = group * N_TRACES_PER_GROUP
    for i in range(start, start + N_TRACES_PER_GROUP):
        out[i] = True
    return out


# ---------- two button rows, one per dollar basis ----------
# Each button is a complete (year, mode) selector. Clicking a button in
# Row 0 (nominal) ALSO deactivates Row 1's highlight via
# "updatemenus[1].active": -1, and vice versa — so at any moment, exactly
# one button across both rows is highlighted, and the chart and the
# highlight always agree. No dynamic caption needed (the active button
# is the source of truth for the current view).

NOMINAL_Y_TITLES = {
    "yaxis.title.text": "Price, $/cwt (nominal)",
    "yaxis2.title.text": "Price, $/cwt (nominal)",
}
REAL_Y_TITLES = {
    "yaxis.title.text": f"Price, $/cwt (base = {cpi_basis_label})",
    "yaxis2.title.text": f"Price, $/cwt (base = {cpi_basis_label})",
}

# Row 0 — nominal year buttons
nominal_row_buttons = []
for y_idx, (y_key, y_label, lo_d, hi_d) in enumerate(YEAR_WINDOWS):
    nominal_row_buttons.append(dict(
        label=y_label,
        method="update",
        args=[
            {"visible": _visibility_for(y_idx, 0)},
            {
                **NOMINAL_Y_TITLES,
                "updatemenus[1].active": -1,  # clear real-row highlight
            },
        ],
    ))

# Row 1 — real year buttons
real_row_buttons = []
for y_idx, (y_key, y_label, lo_d, hi_d) in enumerate(YEAR_WINDOWS):
    real_row_buttons.append(dict(
        label=y_label,
        method="update",
        args=[
            {"visible": _visibility_for(y_idx, 1)},
            {
                **REAL_Y_TITLES,
                "updatemenus[0].active": -1,  # clear nominal-row highlight
            },
        ],
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    height=860,
    # Top margin needs to accommodate two button rows + their labels above
    # the subplot title (which sits near y=1.02 in paper coordinates).
    # 200px / 860px = 0.233 paper fraction of vertical space above the
    # plot area, leaving room for buttons at y up to ~1.22.
    margin=dict(t=200, b=60, l=80, r=30),
    hovermode="x unified",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    font=dict(family="Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif", size=13, color="#222"),
    # APPEND to the make_subplots-generated subplot titles ("NM Clovis — Steers",
    # "NM Clovis — Heifers"); a bare `annotations=[...]` would REPLACE them and
    # the panel headers would silently disappear.
    annotations=list(fig.layout.annotations) + [
        dict(text="<b>Nominal $</b>",
             showarrow=False, xref="paper", yref="paper",
             x=0.0, y=1.22, xanchor="left", yanchor="top",
             font=dict(size=12, color="#222")),
        dict(text=f"<b>Real ({cpi_basis_label} $)</b>",
             showarrow=False, xref="paper", yref="paper",
             x=0.0, y=1.10, xanchor="left", yanchor="top",
             font=dict(size=12, color="#222")),
    ],
    updatemenus=[
        dict(  # nominal row (index 0)
            type="buttons",
            direction="right",
            active=0,  # default = All × Nominal
            x=0.18, y=1.22,
            xanchor="left", yanchor="top",
            pad=dict(r=6, t=4, b=4),
            showactive=True,
            buttons=nominal_row_buttons,
        ),
        dict(  # real row (index 1)
            type="buttons",
            direction="right",
            active=-1,  # no default highlight (nominal row owns it)
            x=0.18, y=1.10,
            xanchor="left", yanchor="top",
            pad=dict(r=6, t=4, b=4),
            showactive=True,
            buttons=real_row_buttons,
        ),
    ],
)

# Axes. With shared_xaxes=True the x-axis title only displays on the bottom
# panel. Y-axes are matched so the two panels share a price range — the
# honest visual comparison between Steers and Heifers depends on this.
fig.update_xaxes(
    showgrid=False, linecolor="#888", mirror=False, ticks="outside",
)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Weight (pounds)", row=2, col=1)

fig.update_yaxes(
    showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5",
    tickprefix="$", linecolor="#888", mirror=False, ticks="outside",
    title_text="Price, $/cwt (nominal)",
)
# Match yaxis2 to yaxis so both panels scale identically.
fig.update_layout(yaxis2=dict(matches="y"))

fig.show()
```

## Download

- [Parquet snapshot](../data/processed/clovis_latest.parquet) — Clovis feeder-cattle archive, latest MARS vintage
- [Vintage manifest](../data/processed/clovis_MANIFEST.json) — SHA-256 of every snapshot the pipeline has committed
- [CPI deflator](../data/processed/cpi_latest.parquet) — BLS CPI-U NSA (`CUUR0000SA0`), the deflation basis

## Why these choices

**Min–mean–max rather than a single point.** Auction prices vary within a weight class and within a sale day; collapsing them to a single number hides risk that matters to a seller deciding when to market.

**Nominal and real dollars, reader-selectable.** Many producers think in nominal dollars (what the check actually read). Researchers think in real dollars (to compare 2019 against 2026 without inflation muddying the picture). The toggle lets either audience see what they're looking for. Real-dollar prices use December 2025 as a fixed basis month so historical chart values stay stable across BLS revisions, computed from the BLS CPI-U All Items NSA series, auto-refreshed monthly by `pipelines/bls/`. See [Methodology → CPI](methodology/cpi.qmd).

**Clovis, Medium-and-Large 1, only.** M&L 1 is the dominant grade combination by volume at this auction and across the four-corners region, and the standard reference grade in USDA-AMS feeder-cattle reporting and agricultural-economics analysis of price-slide behavior; restricting to it keeps the comparison apples-to-apples week over week. Additional frames and muscle grades are preserved in the snapshot and can be surfaced in later Phase 2 filters.

## See also

- [Weekly seasonality](seasonality.qmd) — same data, sliced by week of the calendar year instead of by weight class. Answers *"when in the year do these prices peak?"*
- [Methodology → Clovis pipeline](methodology/clovis.qmd) — every filter, bin, and edge case behind the snapshot this page reads from.
- [Methodology → CPI deflation](methodology/cpi.qmd) — how the December-2025-basis deflator is computed.

## What's next on this page

- [x] Real Clovis data (live MARS feed) replacing the synthetic stand-in.
- [x] CPI deflation toggle (nominal / real December-2025 dollars).
- [x] Companion seasonality page (matches article Figures 3–4).
- [ ] Year-range slider + class filter (Phase 2).
- [ ] Historical reconstruction extending the window back to 1992 (separate workstream).
- [ ] Downloadable session summary for producers to take to a banker (Phase 3).
